name: Hearty Stovetop Beef Stew
recipeIngredient: 4 lbs. boneless beef chuck, well-trimmed, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 cup dry red wine or water
1 1/4 lbs. carrots, peeled and cut crosswise into 1 1/4-inch chunks
1 1/4 lb. small white turnips, trimmed and cut into 1-inch thick wedges
1 bag (16 oz.) frozen cut green beans
1 bag (16 oz.) frozen small whole onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour and salt.
2. Coat meat, shaking off excess. Heat oil in a 5 or 6 quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat until it looks thin and rippling but not smoking.
3. Add beef in 2 or 3 batches; brown on all sides. Remove to bowl with slotted spoon.
4. When all beef is browned, pour off any drippings remaining in the pot.
5. Return browned meat to pot; stir in remaining ingredients except vegetables.
6. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer for 1 hour.
7. Add carrots and turnips. Simmer covered for 45 minutes or until carrots are almost tender.
8. Stir in remaining ingredients. Cover and simmer for an additional 45 minutes until vegetables and meat are tender, stirring occasionally.
9. Cool completely.
10. Pack, label, and freeze up to 3 months. For thicker gravy, mix 3 tablespoons flour and 1/4 cup water. Stir into stew and cook over medium heat about 5 minutes until thickened, stirring constantly.
